title: python programming

scenes.narration: In Python, variables are like named containers that hold data. Let's imagine a variable named 'score' holding the value 100.
scenes.visual: The text 'score = 100' appears on screen, representing a variable assignment.

scenes.narration: We can easily change the value a variable holds. Here, 'score' is updated to 150, demonstrating that variables are mutable.
scenes.visual: The value of the 'score' variable changes from 100 to 150.

scenes.narration: Python supports various data types. For example, 'age' is an integer, 'name' is a string, and 'is_active' is a boolean. Each type has specific properties and uses.
scenes.visual: Examples of integer, string, and boolean variables with their respective values and type labels are shown.

manim_code:
from manim import *

class GeneratedScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Scene 1: Introduce variable assignment
        var_name = Text("score", font_size=48, color=BLUE)
        assign_op = Text("=", font_size=48, color=WHITE)
        value_1 = Text("100", font_size=48, color=YELLOW)

        initial_assignment = VGroup(var_name, assign_op, value_1).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.5)
        explanation_1 = Text("score = 100 (Integer)", font_size=30).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(Write(var_name))
        self.play(Write(assign_op))
        self.play(Write(value_1))
        self.play(Write(explanation_1))
        self.wait(1.5)

        # Scene 2: Re-assign variable
        value_2 = Text("150", font_size=48, color=YELLOW).move_to(value_1.get_center())
        explanation_2 = Text("score = 150 (Variable Reassignment)", font_size=30).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(Transform(value_1, value_2))
        self.play(Transform(explanation_1, explanation_2))
        self.wait(1.5)

        # Scene 3: Show different data types
        self.play(FadeOut(initial_assignment, explanation_1))

        # Integer example
        int_var_text = Text("age = 30", font_size=40, color=BLUE).shift(UP * 1.5 + LEFT * 1.5)
        int_type_label = Text("Integer", font_size=28, color=YELLOW).next_to(int_var_text, RIGHT, buff=0.5)
        int_group = VGroup(int_var_text, int_type_label)

        # String example
        str_var_text = Text('name = "Alice"', font_size=40, color=BLUE).shift(LEFT * 1.5)
        str_type_label = Text("String", font_size=28, color=YELLOW).next_to(str_var_text, RIGHT, buff=0.5)
        str_group = VGroup(str_var_text, str_type_label)

        # Boolean example
        bool_var_text = Text("is_active = True", font_size=40, color=BLUE).shift(DOWN * 1.5 + LEFT * 1.5)
        bool_type_label = Text("Boolean", font_size=28, color=YELLOW).next_to(bool_var_text, RIGHT, buff=0.5)
        bool_group = VGroup(bool_var_text, bool_type_label)

        explanation_3 = Text("Python supports various data types.", font_size=30).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(Write(int_group))
        self.wait(0.5)
        self.play(Write(str_group))
        self.wait(0.5)
        self.play(Write(bool_group))
        self.wait(0.5)
        self.play(Write(explanation_3))
        self.wait(2)
